ip_risky.brand: Anonymous freelance client / poster studio (no client brand name); poster subjects named as Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, and Kylian Mbappé around a fictional "Continental Cup" trophy
ip_risky.note: The full_brief and one_line_ask explicitly name three real, living, world-famous footballers — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, and Kylian Mbappé — as the depicted subjects of a commercial poster. These are real people with right-of-publicity/likeness protection, so the brief as delivered commissions grading a poster built around named celebrity likenesses. The grounding_note and input gen_prompts try to launder this ("faces generic, not actual celebrities", no real flags/logos, fictional Continental Cup), and the actual generated assets are generic/unbranded — but the brief text the agent receives still names the three real players. This is the same category as using a real person's name/likeness; it should not be flagged only if it were a fictional brand, and here real individuals are named.

ip_risky.brand: John Paul the Great Academy (a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana)
ip_risky.note: The client is NOT fictional. The spec's own grounding_note states the brand is 'VERBATIM' from a real Upwork brief ('cand_52'): 'John Paul the Great Academy, a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana' — a specific, named, geographically-pinned real religious institution whose name also invokes a real historical/religious figure (Pope John Paul II, 'John Paul the Great'). Real 'John Paul the Great' Catholic schools/academies exist. The benchmark requires the CLIENT to be an invented/fictional brand; using a verbatim real school (name + denomination + city + founding year 2006 + anniversary) violates that rule and carries real-org/trademark and sensitivity risk. Delivery-platform allowances (Upwork/Firefly) do not apply — the flagged entity is the client itself.

ip_risky.brand: THE LONG WAY ROUND (a YouTube travel/road-trip podcast, mustard-and-teal campervan logo)
ip_risky.note: The client brand "THE LONG WAY ROUND" with a road-trip / campervan travel theme is confusingly close to the well-known real travel-media franchise "Long Way Round" (Ewan McGregor & Charley Boorman's 2004 series, which spawned Long Way Down / Long Way Up). As a branded travel/road-trip media property it reads as an existing franchise rather than an invented client. Recommend renaming the fictional podcast brand to something unambiguous (e.g. "Wander Miles", "Detour Diaries").

"Beacon Mutual Insurance Group" is confusingly close to a REAL company: Beacon Mutual Insurance Company is an actual, well-known Rhode Island insurance carrier (workers' comp). The core mark "Beacon Mutual Insurance" is nearly identical and in the exact same industry (insurance); adding "Group" and switching the line to personal auto does not clear the collision. Should be renamed to a clearly invented, non-colliding brand.

doable_issues.note: Step 10 (image_fill_area to "place the white-bg hero onto a solid pure-#FFFFFF square 1:1 canvas... extends the white field to a square") is not connector-doable: image_fill_area is a SOLID-color fill of a selection WITHIN the existing canvas — it cannot extend/grow the canvas or composite an image onto a larger square canvas. Squaring a non-square image requires canvas padding/extension, which only the banned image_generative_expand does. The reverify explicitly swapped the banned generative_expand for image_fill_area, but image_fill_area physically cannot produce a square 2048x2048 canvas from the non-square source. All other 15 steps are real, doable [C] Adobe actions.

Step 16 (banner) uses image_fill_area to "place the graded ~2400x2400 square master on a solid white 16:9 2560x1440 canvas, bracelet offset, with text-safe negative space." image_fill_area is solid-color fill of a selection WITHIN an existing image — explicitly "NOT a compositor" — and cannot enlarge/re-shape a square into a wider-than-source canvas with the subject offset and added white space. That is canvas extension = image_generative_expand (banned) or local compositing. The reverify note itself admits step 16 was an image_generative_expand outpaint "replaced by image_fill_area" — a mislabel, not a real fix. Its output spec (2560x1440, wider than the 2400 source) is unachievable with fill alone. All other 20 steps are valid, correctly-ordered headless connector actions.

Executable connector_workflow is fully doable — all 22 steps are real Adobe connector actions; step 18 hero-banner uses image_crop_and_resize in reframe mode (gen-free, no invented pixels), image_fill_area is solid-white fill only, document_render_layout only EXPORTS a client-authored .indd, and the literal 3D-CAD ask is correctly re-anchored out of scope. BUT the outputs[] spec for hero_banner_wide still literally reads "Generative-expand (outpaint) widening of the corrected hero" — stale text describing a BANNED capability that the workflow and reverify already replaced with a reframe crop. The chain itself is clean; the leftover description is a documentation inconsistency that should be reconciled.

Step 9 (image_fill_area) is assigned a canvas-EXTENSION job it cannot do: the note says it "places the popped hero on a solid near-black charcoal brand canvas at the wider 16:9 target" with new dark negative space on the LEFT. image_fill_area is solid-color fill of an existing region only (select→[invert]→fill) — it cannot enlarge the frame from the 3:2 source to a wider 16:9 or add new left-side space. This is the leftover 1:1 swap of the removed banned image_generative_expand; the tool is allowed but mis-tasked. Workaroundable (crop_and_resize in step 10 can reach 16:9 with the subject offset so the dark surround already reads as left negative space), so not a hard blocker. All other 21 steps are real, headless [C] Adobe actions with no banned capability (no gen-fill/outpaint/upscale/text-to-image/object-removal-reconstruction).

No BANNED capability remains (prior generative_expand was swapped out; vectorize/halftone/remove_bg/solid-fill are all allowed and headless). But two connector-fidelity gaps: (1) Step 14 uses image_fill_area to build a "wide banner / social-header lockup" from a square mark — image_fill_area only fills a masked region of an EXISTING image with solid color; it cannot expand/create a differently-sized canvas or composite a transparent foreground onto a new banner-sized background, so this deliverable is not achievable with the named tool (a true wide-banner would need canvas expansion = banned, or a layout/composite tool). (2) Step 12's single-color recolor uses the select_subject→invert_selection→fill_area chain that the image_fill_area docs explicitly warn against (transparent-PNG-as-mask = wrong polarity). Also step 11 image_invert_selection is a dead/unused step (spec self-admits).

The executable connector_workflow is fully doable: n=8 was correctly fixed from image_generative_expand to image_crop_and_resize (reframe only). BUT stale banned-capability language survives in descriptive fields: the hero_property_banner OUTPUT spec (line 60) still requires "canvas extended via generative outpaint on sky/foreground," and chaining_note (line 329) still says "generative_expand consumes it for the banner (8)." The deliverable spec thus still demands a banned generative-outpaint result even though the workflow no longer produces one.

Step 11 image_apply_lens_blur is fed a background mask to 'blur only the masked background,' but CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.md line 44 tags lens_blur as '(whole-image, no mask)'. The subject-pop/blur-background treatment IS doable via the documented image_apply_preset masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' (line 43), so the deliverable is achievable but the specified path is wrong. No banned capability is used (generative_expand explicitly avoided; solid-fill only; correct stock license-before-download order). Separately, the '5 graded frames' output is only costed for the hero (steps 3-8); the other 4 are an admitted uncosted 'implied batch' and step 19 copies only hero_graded_final, so the call-list under-represents the stated output.

Executable connector_workflow (steps 1-21) is clean and connector-doable — no banned generative step; the old image_generative_expand was replaced by image_fill_area solid-white fill. BUT stale text still prescribes a banned capability: output spec for hero_catalog_banner (line 46) says the banner is made "after generative outpaint gave horizontal breathing room," and difficulty_rationale (line 275) says "The single generative step is the allowed image_generative_expand outpaint" and lists "outpaint" in the pipeline; chaining_note (line 274) names step 11 as "generative_expand(11)". These contradict the actual step 11 and the reverify note. Secondary: step 11 image_fill_area is solid-fill (in-scope, not banned) but is a mild mis-map since fill_area does not literally extend/widen a canvas.

The two .ai inputs (letters_AZ.ai_authored, numbers_09.ai_authored) fed to document_render_vector in steps 20/21 are neither in inputs[] (only 3 raster images) nor produced by any prior connector step — image_vectorize emits .svg, not .ai, and no connector converts SVG→.ai. So the .ai and .eps deliverables depend on an off-connector authoring/re-wrap step the spec discloses but never realizes with a connector. SVG deliverables (steps 11/19) are fully doable; AI/EPS half is not connector-produced. NOTE: reverify's headline claim that EPS is not a render_vector capability is FALSE — the tool schema explicitly lists application/eps as a supported outputMediaType.

No banned-capability violation (from-scratch folk-art art is correctly supplied as an approved input, not generated; image_fill_area is solid white preset fill; render_vector only exports the authored separations.ai; no text-to-image/gen-fill/object-removal/upscaling). But two connector-accuracy slips, both self-noted in reverify: step 7 image_vectorize has no maskURI param so the lettering_mask input is inert (it would trace the whole raster, not just lettering — an intermediate masked-raster step is needed); step 16 claims one render emits PDF+PNG+SVG when render_vector emits one media type per call (3 calls). Also loose print semantics: whole-layer gaussian blur isn't a true dot choke and tint/overlay on one shared field yields colored proofs, not independently-registering spot channels. Feasible as a headless job but not literally a press-ready 5-screen sep pack.

Step 8 (image_fill_area) cannot do what it claims: it fills a masked region of an EXISTING image with solid color within the current canvas — it cannot enlarge/extend a 1024x1024 frame onto a new 1080x1920 canvas or composite the frame onto a larger solid ground. Going square→vertical canvas is a canvas-extension op the connector only offers via image_generative_expand (BANNED). No non-generative solid-fill path achieves this. Worse, the spec still carries live references to the banned capability the reverify claims was removed: output spec line 72 says the cover is "outpainted top+bottom to 9:16"; chaining_note (line 270) says "→ generative_expand to 9:16 (8)"; difficulty_rationale (line 271) says "The one generative step is image_generative_expand (outpaint) only". These contradict the patched workflow and reference a banned tool. All other steps (remove_bg, vectorize, preset/temp/single-color-sat grade, stock license+add_grain, media_enhance_speech [A], video_resize same-length [A], font_recommend, firefly board) are real, doable connector actions.

The executable connector_workflow (steps 1-25) is fully connector-doable — step 10 is image_fill_area (solid charcoal-canvas fill, allowed), NOT generative expand. HOWEVER the hero_banner_still output spec (line 48) still instructs "top-extended via generative outpaint to a tall hero-banner canvas" — a BANNED capability — and chaining_note (line 339) + difficulty_rationale (line 340) still name image_generative_expand/outpaint as the active step. The workflow was fixed but the deliverable-spec/rationale text is stale, contradicting it.

No banned capability (no gen-fill/text-to-image/upscale/object-removal; image_fill_area used in allowed solid-color mode; async video is [A] product-doable). BUT the vector branch is broken: image_vectorize (step 9) emits SVG, then steps 10 image_fill_area and 11 image_crop_and_resize are raster-only tools — feeding an SVG into raster select-and-fill/crop is a type mismatch, so the claimed muscle_arrow_accent.svg vector deliverable cannot actually be produced by the chain. Also step 10 image_fill_area needs a preceding selection (image_select_by_prompt/subject) which is missing.

The runnable connector_workflow is fully doable — step 14 correctly uses image_crop_and_resize (a gen-free reframe), and the reverify block confirms the previously-banned image_generative_expand was swapped out. BUT the output spec for MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg (outputs[5], line 80) still reads "generated by AI-outpainting the graded hero to extended canvas (content extended, original not cropped)" — a banned generative-outpaint description that contradicts the corrected crop/resize workflow step. All 22 connector_workflow steps and tools_used are real Adobe connector actions with no banned capability in the EXECUTABLE chain (the reverify swapped the banned image_generative_expand out for image_fill_area at step 7). Two residual problems: (1) STALE banned-capability prose — output spec line 75 still says the lockup is "outpainted from the square logo composition" and chaining_note line 332 still says "generative_expand→3:1 horizontal lockup (7)", both describing the banned outpaint that was supposedly removed. (2) image_fill_area is a SOLID-color fill only and cannot actually place/composite the logo onto an extended 3:1 brand-blue canvas or reframe canvas dimensions, so step 7's stated deliverable ("place the logo on a solid brand-blue canvas at a 3:1 area") is weak/questionable via that tool. Everything else (vectorize, monochromatic_tint, color_overlay, [T] data-merge on client-authored .indd/.ai, render_layout/vector export, license stock, auto_tone, color_temperature, crop_and_resize, create_firefly_board) is legitimately doable.

Step 10 uses asset_preview_file to pull "only the flagged pages" of the proof PDF into a new subset flagged-pages PDF. Preview tools generate previews of a file; extracting/subsetting specific pages into a new multi-page PDF is a page-selection/split operation the preview tool does not natively perform, so the tool mapping is imperfect. The operation itself is benign and non-generative (no annotations drawn, no new content), so this is a mapping stretch, not a banned capability. All other steps (create folders, upload init/finalize, document_merge_data_layout [T] on client-authored .indd, document_render_layout, document_convert_pdf, asset_preview_file/inline_preview inspect, media_summarize [A], local write/zip) are real connector-doable actions with no banned capability.
